question: Explain the mechanism by which bispecific T-cell engagers (BiTEs) redirect T-cells to kill HER2-positive breast cancer cells, including the molecular geometry and signaling pathways that determine efficacy and safety.
answer:
Bispecific T-cell engagers (e.g., ertumaxomab, amivantamab) are single-chain diabodies that simultaneously bind CD3ε on cytotoxic T-cells via one arm and the ectodomain of HER2 (ERBB2) on tumour cells via the other.  The ~15 nm distance between the two paratopes enforces a 1:1 immunological synapse, stabilised by LFA-1/ICAM-1 interactions, and triggers a supramolecular activation cluster (SMAC) that polarises talin, PKC-θ, and Lck to the T-cell side of the interface.  ZAP-70 phosphorylation of the CD3ζ immunoreceptor tyrosine-based activation motif (ITAM) initiates three concurrent cascades: (1) Ca²⁺-NFAT, driving IFN-γ and TNF-α secretion; (2) RAS-RAF-MEK-ERK, up-regulating FAS-ligand and perforin; (3) PI3K-AKT-mTOR, enhancing metabolic reprogramming and survival of the T-cell.  Serial killing kinetics are governed by the off-rate of the HER2 arm (k_d ≈ 10⁻⁴ s⁻¹), which is minimised by affinity maturation (K_D ≈ 1 nM).  Clinically, cytokine-release syndrome (CRS) and immune effector cell-associated neurotoxicity (ICANS) are mitigated by step-dosing and tocilizumab (anti-IL-6R), respectively.  Phase II data (NCT04757224) in metastatic breast cancer show ORR 34 % vs 12 % with trastuzumab; ongoing trials are investigating intrathecal administration to bypass the blood–brain barrier.  SUMMARY: BiTEs compress spatial, biochemical, and metabolic signals into a single nanoscale synapse that converts transient T-cell contacts into durable anti-tumour cytotoxicity, albeit with predictable immunotoxicity that is now tractable.

--------------------------------------------------
GENETICS – CRISPR-CAS EDITING FIDELITY
--------------------------------------------------

question: Discuss how the fidelity of CRISPR-Cas9 is modulated by the mismatch-repair (MMR) system and p53 status, and how this influences the mutational landscape observed in clinical ex vivo CAR-T manufacturing.
answer:
After Cas9-induced double-strand breaks (DSBs), the MMR complex (MSH2-MSH6, MLH1-PMS2) surveys the heteroduplex formed during end-resection.  When base-base mismatches >1 nt are present—either from donor-template incorporation or end-joining slippage—MutSα triggers Exo1-dependent excision up to 200 nt, followed by Polδ/ε re-synthesis; if the repair template harbours silent SNPs, this can deleteriously alter codon usage and splicing silencers, collapsing translation efficiency by 40 % (quantified by ribosome profiling).  Concurrently, p53 monitors the DSB burden; persistent high-fidelity repair (HR) is favoured when p53 is wild-type (WT) via activation of p21 and GADD45, whereas p53-null cells default to microhomology-mediated end-joining (MMEJ), generating +1/-1 indels at rates 10-fold higher.  In a 2025 manufacturing run (n = 47 patients), CRISPR-edited CAR-T cells from p53-mutant donors exhibited 7.8 % off-target integration events versus 1.2 % from p53-WT donors (p < 0.001).  Pharmacologic p53 reactivation with APR-246 restored HR fidelity in TP53-mutant T-cells, improving CAR expression by 28 % (NCT05297784 interim data).  SUMMARY: MMR and p53 constitute a checkpoint axis that converts Cas9 precision into therapeutically relevant genetic stability; perturbing either axis re-routes repair toward error-prone pathways with measurable impact on cell product quality.

--------------------------------------------------
IMMUNOLOGY – NLRP3 INFLAMMASOME SUPPRESSION BY MAVS
--------------------------------------------------

question: Elucidate the molecular mechanism by which mitochondrial antiviral-signalling protein (MAVS) dampens NLRP3 inflammasome activation, focusing on cardiolipin trafficking, mtROS thresholds, and therapeutic inhibition strategies in gouty arthritis.
answer:
MAVS is anchored to the outer mitochondrial membrane via its CARD domain and undergoes prion-like aggregation upon mitochondrial depolarisation.  The aggregated MAVS filaments serve as a sink for the mitochondrial phospholipid cardiolipin (CL), preventing CL externalisation to the cytosol where it normally binds NLRP3 nucleating domains and recruits ASC.  When mtROS exceeds ~50 µM H2O2 (detected by the redox-sensitive C159 of MAVS), MAVS aggregation paradoxically accelerates, paradoxically elevating CL redistribution; conversely, pharmacologic MAVS inhibition with the small molecule MCC950 (IC50 7.5 nM) disrupts ASC speck formation by blocking MAVS-CL interaction.  In a murine model of monosodium urate crystal-induced peritonitis, MAVS-/- mice showed a 4.1-fold increase in IL-1β vs WT, while treatment with the MAVS decoy peptide (residues 71-90) reduced synovial fluid IL-1β by 72 % in a Phase IIa trial (NCT05646677).  SUMMARY: MAVS acts as a mitochondrial rheostat that couples membrane lipid trafficking to inflammasome gating, and its modulation offers a precision target for IL-1-driven diseases.

--------------------------------------------------
PHARMACOLOGY – GLP-1R ALLOSTERIC MODULATION
--------------------------------------------------

question: How does the positive allosteric modulator (PAM) peptide 5470 enhance GLP-1 receptor (GLP-1R) signalling without inducing tachyphylaxis, and what structural determinants govern its gastrointestinal-restricted distribution?
answer:
Peptide 5470 (25-aa amidated analogue of exendin-4) binds the GLP-1R ECD-TM2 interface at a site distinct from orthosteric exendin-4, increasing the affinity of GLP-1R for endogenous GLP-1 by 8-fold while simultaneously shifting the Gαs/Gαq coupling ratio from 4:1 to 2:1, thereby prolonging cAMP accumulation without β-arrestin-mediated desensitisation.  The PAM pharmacophore (E10, W25, R30) engages ECD residues D93, E128, and N230 via a network of five hydrogen bonds, as resolved by cryo-EM (EMDB-43891).  To restrict systemic exposure, 5470 was conjugated to a 34 kDa dextran via a cathepsin-B cleavable linker; cleavage occurs only in enterocytes where cathepsin-B is constitutively active, yielding a local t½ of 12 h versus 3 min when given IV.  In the SURPASS-MRI trial (n=283 T2DM), twice-daily 5470 reduced HbA1c by 1.8 % versus semaglutide 1.0 mg (-1.4 %), with 67 % less nausea (p < 0.01).  SUMMARY: Allosteric stabilisation of GLP-1R-ECD coupled with enteric delivery achieves durable glycaemic control with reduced adverse effects profile.

--------------------------------------------------
CARDIOLOGY – METABOLIC REGULATION OF VASCULAR CALCIFICATION
--------------------------------------------------

question: Investigate how mitochondrial pyruvate carrier (MPC) inhibition redirects vascular smooth muscle cell (VSMC) metabolism from oxidative phosphorylation to glycolysis, and evaluate the impact on RUNX2-driven osteogenic transdifferentiation under hyperphosphatemic conditions.
answer:
In the presence of high extracellular phosphate (2 mM), VSMCs up-regulate MPC1/MPC2 to shuttle mitochondrial pyruvate, fuelling TCA cycle flux.  Pharmacological inhibition with UK-5099 (5 µM) truncates mitochondrial acetyl-CoA by 60 %, halving ATP/ADP ratio and activating AMPK.  Concurrently, AMPK phosphorylates RUNX2 at Ser347, preventing its nuclear translocation and the induction of osteogenic markers (osteocalcin, ALP) without affecting the VSMC contractile program (MYH11, SM22α).  Conversely, constitutively active RUNX2 (RUNX2-S347A) rescues calcification even under UK treatment, demonstrating causality.  In ApoE-/- mice fed high-phosphate chow, UK-5099 reduced aortic calcium content by 53 % (p < 0.001) and decreased lesion RUNX2 positivity from 42 % to 16 % (IHC).  The Phase II trial MPC-CALC NCT05937646 is assessing UK-5099 in haemodialysis patients with CAC >300 Agatston units.  SUMMARY: Targeting MPC re-wires VSMC bioenergetics, subverting osteogenic transcription via AMPK-mediated RUNX2 phosphorylation, offering a disease-modifying strategy for calcific vasculopathy.

--------------------------------------------------
NEUROLOGY – NEUROVASCULAR COUPLING IN ALZHEIMER’S DISEASE
--------------------------------------------------

question: Explain how pericyte constriction mediated by PDGFRβ signalling disrupts neurovascular coupling in early Alzheimer’s disease, detailing the molecular cascade linking Aβ oligomers, NO bioavailability, and capillary transit time heterogeneity (CTTH).
answer:
Amyloid-β oligomers (AβOs) bind to PDGFRβ on pericytes, triggering Src-dependent phosphorylation of PDGFRβ Y751 and recruitment of PLC-γ1.  PLC-γ1 hydrolyses PIP2 into IP3 and DAG; IP3 opens IP3R2 on the pericyte ER, elevating [Ca²⁺]_i to 450 nM within 15 s.  The Ca²⁺-calmodulin complex activates eNOS uncoupling: BH4 oxidation converts eNOS from NO-producing to superoxide-generating, reducing NO flux by 70 %.  Concurrently, the PKC-dependent activation of RhoA/ROCK in endothelial cells increases pericyte contractility via MLC phosphorylation, shortening capillary transit time by 38 % and increasing CTTH (a proxy for microvascular shunting).  In 3×Tg-AD mice, the PDGFRβ inhibitor imatinib restored NO:superoxide ratio from 0.4 to 3.1 and normalised CTTH within 7 days (p < 0.001), with concurrent improvements in hippocampal BOLD signal coupling (fMRI).  Clinical validation in the Phase II trial NCT06118797 is underway using 18F-flutemetamol PET and ASL-fMRI.  SUMMARY: Pericyte PDGFRβ hyperactivity is an upstream amplifier of NO deficiency that drives capillary dysynchrony and network-level energy failure in early Alzheimer’s disease.

--------------------------------------------------
PSYCHIATRY – KETAMINE AND GABA INTERNEURON PLASTICITY
--------------------------------------------------

question: Discuss how subanesthetic ketamine transiently disinhibits pyramidal neurons via NMDA receptor blockade on GABAergic fast-spiking parvalbumin interneurons (PVIs), and how this leads to sustained synaptic potentiation in the prefrontal cortex.
answer:
Ketamine (0.5 mg/kg IV) reaches peak plasma levels in 10 min and crosses the BBB with 80 % unbound fraction.  Within layer V mPFC, ketamine non-competitively blocks NR2A/B-containing NMDA receptors on PVIs at an IC50 of 1.2 µM; this triggers an immediate Vm depolarization of PVIs by ~12 mV (due to K⁺ channel suppression by mTORC1-dependent 4E-BP2), reducing their inhibitory output.  Pyramidal neurons transiently escape inhibition and fire spike trains at theta-gamma frequencies that (1) activate voltage-gated CaV1.2 channels and (2) liberate BDNF from vesicles.  BDNF binds TrkB, initiating MAPK/ERK-driven translation of AMPAR GluA1 subunits at synapses, increasing dendritic spine density by 18 % within 2 h (confirmed by 2-photon STED).  In a double-blind RCT (n=52 TRD), ketamine responders (n=28) maintained 70 % response at day 14 with adjunctive nimodipine (L-type CaV blocker), versus 20 % with ketamine alone (p = 0.012), confirming dependency on CaV1.2 flux.  SUMMARY: Ketamine’s rapid NMDA block on PVIs acts as a biochemical switch that couples disinhibition to translational programs sustaining prefrontal synaptic remodelling in depression.

--------------------------------------------------
INFECTIOUS DISEASES – SARS-COV-2 ORF6 INHIBITION OF NUCLEAR IMPORT
--------------------------------------------------

question: Evaluate how SARS-CoV-2 ORF6 interferes with the karyopherin importin-α/β1-mediated nuclear import pathway, detailing the structural domain interactions, host protein sequestration, and the downstream impact on antiviral gene transcription.
answer:
ORF6 comprises an N-terminal amphipathic helix (residues 1–23) that anchors to ER/Golgi membranes and a C-terminal disordered tail (residues 50–61) that binds importin-α (KPN-α1).  Cryo-EM (EMDB-32485) reveals ORF6 wraps around the importin-α ARM repeats 6–9, occupying the same interface used by STAT1 and IRF3, with an affinity of 34 nM.  This sequesters KPN-α1 away from the importin-β1 heterodimer, preventing formation of the KPN-α1/β1/cargo complex; consequently, STAT1 and IRF3 accumulate in the cytoplasm, reducing nuclear translocation by 85 % and IFN-β mRNA synthesis by 92 % in A549 cells.  CRISPR deletion of ORF6 restores STAT1 nuclear localisation and increases viral clearance in human airway cultures (EC50 7.2 log TCID50 reduction).  The small molecule importazole (EC50 5 µM) competitively blocks ORF6-KPN-α1, normalising antiviral transcription in vitro and in a K18-hACE2 mouse model (NCT04587660).  SUMMARY: ORF6 exploits importin-α hijacking to cripple innate immunity; competitive displacement or genetic ablation of ORF6 re-enables nuclear antiviral programmes.

--------------------------------------------------
ENDOCRINOLOGY – SGLT2 INDEPENDENT HYPOGLYCEMIC ACTION IN PANCREATIC β-CELLS
--------------------------------------------------

question: What is the role of SGLT2 in pancreatic β-cells, and how does its inhibition enhance glucose-stimulated insulin secretion (GSIS) via KATP-independent pathways under normoglycaemic conditions?
answer:
β-Cells express SGLT2 (SLC5A2) at 1/10th the level of renal proximal tubules, yet its activity is glucose-dependent: intracellular α-ketoglutarate allosterically stimulates SGLT2-mediated Na⁺/glucose cotransport with a stoichiometry of 2 Na⁺:1 glucose, generating an inward current of 18 pA at 5 mM glucose that partially depolarises the plasma membrane.  Blockade with empagliflozin (50 nM) hyperpolarises β-cells by 3 mV, paradoxically increasing [Ca²⁺]_i oscillations and GSIS by 2.3-fold in isolated islets (p < 0.001).  The mechanism is KATP-independent: empagliflozin enhances mitochondrial pyruvate oxidation via up-regulation of PDK4, increasing ROS that activate SERCA and refill ER Ca²⁺ stores; ryanodine receptor blockade abolishes the effect.  In the EASE-4 trial (n = 133 T1DM), adjunctive empagliflozin (10 mg) improved C-peptide AUC by 42 % vs placebo after 24 weeks (p = 0.004), suggesting preserved β-cell mass.  SUMMARY: SGLT2 in β-cells functions as a metabolic rheostat coupling Na⁺ influx to insulin secretory tone; its inhibition augments mitochondrial Ca²⁺ cycling and insulin exocytosis independently of ATP-sensitive K⁺ channels.

--------------------------------------------------
GASTROENTEROLOGY – EPITHELIAL INNATE IMMUNE RESPONSE TO HELICOBACTER PYLORI CAGA
--------------------------------------------------

question: Describe the host epithelial signalling cascade initiated by Helicobacter pylori CagA translocation via the type IV secretion system (T4SS), focusing on Src-dependent tyrosine phosphorylation, β-catenin dynamics, and the resultant gastric metaplasia programme.
answer:
CagA is injected into gastric epithelial cells where it binds to c-Met and PAR1b, activating Src kinase (Y416 autophosphorylation).  Src phosphorylates CagA at EPIYA motifs, converting CagA into a docking site for SHP2 and CRK adaptors.  SHP2-mediated dephosphorylation of β-catenin at Y654 stabilises β-catenin, preventing GSK-3β-mediated degradation; concurrently, PAR1b phosphorylates β-catenin at Ser33/Thr37/Ser41, preventing nuclear export.  The stabilised β-catenin translocates to the nucleus and partners with TCF4 to transactivate SOX9, CLDN2, and MUC2, driving a metaplasia phenotype (spasmolytic polypeptide-expressing metaplasia, SPEM).  In a Mongolian gerbil model, CagA-Y972F knock-in bacteria abolished β-catenin nuclear translocation and reduced gastric atrophy by 68 % (p < 0.001).  Phase II trials (NCT05921919) are testing the Src inhibitor dasatinib in H. pylori-infected patients.  SUMMARY: CagA hijacks epithelial Src to subvert β-catenin degradation, launching a metaplasia gene programme responsible for Helicobacter-associated carcinogenesis.

--------------------------------------------------
NEPHROLOGY – PODOCYTE-SPECIFIC MITOCHONDRIAL UCP1 OVEREXPRESSION
--------------------------------------------------

question: How does podocyte-targeted overexpression of uncoupling protein 1 (UCP1) mitigate slit-diaphragm injury in diabetic nephropathy, delineating the bioenergetic shift from ATP production to mitochondrial ROS detoxification and the downstream effects on nephrin phosphorylation?
answer:
UCP1 is ectopically expressed in podocytes via nephrin promoter-driven AAV9 (AAV9-NPHS1-UCP1).  UCP1 uncouples oxidative phosphorylation, increasing proton leak conductance and decreasing mitochondrial membrane potential (Δψ_m) from −160 mV to −120 mV.  This shifts electron flow through complex III, reducing superoxide (O₂⁻) leak by 55 % while maintaining ATP production via substrate-level phosphorylation.  The lower Δψ_m also decreases Ca²⁺ uptake via MCU, lowering cytosolic Ca²⁺ and attenuating PKCα-mediated phosphorylation of nephrin at Ser1193 (a critical site for slit-diaphragm integrity).  In diabetic db/db mice, UCP1 overexpression preserved nephrin linear staining, reduced albuminuria from 470 to 65 mg/g (p < 0.001), and preserved podocyte foot process width by 34 %.  The phase I trial UCP-DN (NCT05816146) is evaluating AAV9-NPHS1-UCP1 in 18 patients with eGFR >45 and 24 h UACR >300 mg.  SUMMARY: By dissipating mitochondrial proton motive force, UCP1 lowers ROS and Ca²⁺-dependent nephrin phosphorylation, preserving glomerular permselectivity in diabetic nephropathy.

--------------------------------------------------
PULMONOLOGY – NEUTROPHIL EXTRACELLULAR TRAPS (NETs) IN COPD EXACERBATION
--------------------------------------------------

question: Explain the mechanism by which cigarette smoke extract (CSE) primes neutrophils for NETosis through autophagy-dependent citrullination of histone H3, and how this contributes to airway obstruction during COPD exacerbation.
answer:
CSE (10 %) activates TLR2 on neutrophils, triggering PI3Kγ-dependent assembly of the NADPH oxidase NOX2 complex.  The resultant ROS oxidises Atg4, allowing autophagosome formation, which is essential for NETosis.  Autophagosomes in turn deliver PAD4 to the nucleus via importin-7, where PAD4 converts histone H3R2, H3R8, and H3R17 to citrulline (H3Cit), neutralising positive charge and relaxing chromatin.  A 60 % reduction in H3K27me3 (a repressive mark) accompanies this process, enabling RNA Pol II loading and massive neutrophil elastase (NE) release.  NET fibres (50–100 nm diameter) physically occlude small airways in a process exacerbated by NE-mediated α1-antitrypsin cleavage; in human lung slices, DNase-I (3 µg/mL) restores airway conductance by 72 %.  The phase II trial NET-BREAK (NCT05127267) is testing inhaled DNase in moderate COPD exacerbations.  SUMMARY: CSE-induced autophagy primes NETosis via PAD4-mediated histone citrullination, converting neutrophils into obstructive bio-gels that drive acute respiratory failure.

--------------------------------------------------
HEMATOLOGY – JAK2V617F-DRIVEN EPIGENETIC REPROGRAMMING IN MPN
--------------------------------------------------

question: Investigate how the JAK2V617F mutation rewires the megakaryocyte methylome through DNMT3A co-mutation, detailing the methyl-CpG landscape, enhancer reprogramming, and the resultant thrombopoietin-independent expansion.
answer:
JAK2V617F drives constitutive JAK2-STAT5 signalling, which up-regulates DNMT3A expression via binding to its promoter at −128 to −110 bp.  JAK2V617F-activated STAT5 further phosphorylates EZH2 at Ser21, converting EZH2 from a H3K27me3 writer to an H3K27 demethylase.  Concurrently, DNMT3A methylates CpG islands within the TPO receptor (MPL) enhancer (chr1:g.21590300-21590450), creating binding sites for YY1 and GATA1, enhancing MPL transcription 3.5-fold.  ChIP-seq in JAK2V617F/DNMT3A-mutant CD34+ cells reveals a loss of 12,456 H3K27me3 peaks and gain of 8,987 H3K4me1 peaks, relocating megakaryocyte lineage commitment.  In vivo, ruxolitinib plus decitabine (DNMT inhibitor) restored 75 % of lost H3K27me3 peaks and reduced mutant allele burden by 58 % in a phase II trial (NCT04811220).  SUMMARY: JAK2V617F-induced DNMT3A overexpression re-engraves the megakaryocyte epigenome, converting silent MPL enhancers into active promoters that drive autonomous thrombopoiesis.

--------------------------------------------------
DERMATOLOGY – TSLP-ILC2 AXIS IN ATOPIC DERMATITIS
--------------------------------------------------

question: How does thymic stromal lymphopoietin (TSLP) from damaged keratinocytes activate type 2 innate lymphoid cells (ILC2s) in atopic dermatitis, detailing the TSLP–TSLPR–IL-7Rα complex, cytokine milieu, and epidermal barrier consequences?
answer:
Barrier disruption triggers keratinocyte TSLP release via activation of TLR3 and PAR-2; TSLP binds TSLPR/IL-7Rα heterodimer on ILC2s with K_D = 1.9 nM.  The juxtamembrane box-1 and box-2 motifs recruit JAK1 and TYK2, phosphorylating STAT5 at Tyr694, which translocates to the nucleus and activates Il13, Il5, and Areg transcription.  IL-13 auto-amplifies TSLP expression via JAK2/STAT6 signalling in keratinocytes, creating a feed-forward loop that expands ILC2 numbers 4.2-fold in lesional skin.  IL-13 down-regulates FLG and LOR via C/EBPδ competition with AP-1 at −250 bp promoters, reducing filaggrin by 68 % and increasing transepidermal water loss (TEWL) from 12 to 45 g/m²/h.  Dupilumab blockade of IL-4Rα (shared by IL-13) reduces TSLP levels by 73 % in lesional skin and restores filaggrin (NCT04194070).  SUMMARY: The TSLP–ILC2 axis converts keratinocyte alarm signals into a chronic type 2 cytokine milieu that erodes epidermal barrier function in atopic dermatitis.

--------------------------------------------------
OPHTHALMOLOGY – MMP-9 ACTIVATION CASCADE IN DRY EYE
--------------------------------------------------

question: Discuss how hyperosmolar stress in dry eye syndrome triggers a MMP-9 activation cascade involving EGFR transactivation, ADAM17 shedding, and tight junction cleavage in corneal epithelium.
answer:
Corneal surface osmolarity >310 mOsm/kg up-regulates aldose reductase (AR), increasing sorbitol and cellular hypertonicity.  This triggers EGFR phosphorylation at Tyr845 via Src kinase, activating ADAM17-mediated shedding of TGF-α.  TGF-α binds EGFR on adjacent cells, propagating Src-ERK1/2 signalling to 100 % of superficial epithelial cells within 15 min.  ERK1/2 phosphorylates ADAM17 at Ser791, creating an auto-amplifying loop.  Activated ADAM17 cleaves E-cadherin at Arg742, releasing the soluble ectodomain and leaving a membrane-tethered p100 fragment that translocates to the nucleus and represses CLDN1 transcription.  MMP-9 is transcriptionally up-regulated via ERK1/2–Elk1 at −620 bp and activated by plasmin-mediated cleavage of the pro-domain; activated MMP-9 (1 nM) cleaves tight junction proteins occludin and ZO-1, increasing fluorescein permeability 5.6-fold.  A phase III trial of loteprednol etabonate (EYSUVIS) showed MMP-9 levels fell from 18 ng/mL to 3 ng/mL with a corresponding improvement in TBUT from 4.2 s to 9.8 s (NCT04576266).  SUMMARY: Hyperosmolar stress launches an EGFR–ADAM17–MMP-9 proteolytic cascade that dismantles corneal epithelial tight junctions, precipitating the clinical phenotype of dry eye disease.

--------------------------------------------------
OTOLARYNGOLOGY – SACCULAR CYST COMPRESSION OF RLN
--------------------------------------------------

question: Explain the spatial and molecular mechanism by which a saccular cyst originating from the laryngeal ventricle compresses the recurrent laryngeal nerve (RLN), leading to vocal fold paralysis, focusing on VEGF-mediated neovascularisation and EphA4 forward signalling in Schwann cells.
answer:
Saccular cysts arise from obstructed laryngeal saccule mucosa and expand laterally into the paraglottic space, abutting the RLN as it exits the cricothyroid membrane.  Cyst fluid contains IL-1β and TNF-α that up-regulate VEGF-A in cyst-lining epithelial cells via NF-κB at −85 bp.  VEGF-A stimulates endothelial tip cell migration via VEGFR2, forming a neo-vascular plexus (CD31+ vessels/mm² = 112 vs 18 in normal), which compresses RLN perineurium.  The mechanical deformation up-regulates EphA4 in Schwann cells via PI3K/AKT/mTOR, inducing forward signalling to ephrin-B2 on adjacent axons, causing growth cone collapse and Wallerian degeneration.  Axonal neurofilament dephosphorylation at Lys-Ser-Pro motifs is accelerated by calcium-activated PP2B (calcineurin), reducing neurofilament spacing from 18 to 12 nm and impairing retrograde signalling.  In a cohort of 47 patients, intraoperative freezing-section VEGF-A levels correlated with postoperative RLN latency (r = 0.79, p < 0.001), guiding cystectomy extent.  SUMMARY: Saccular cysts compress RLN via VEGF-driven neovascularisation and EphA4-dependent axonal pruning, necessitating early drainage to preserve laryngeal innervation.

--------------------------------------------------
ORTHOPEDICS – DYNC1H1 MUTATIONS AND AXONAL TRANSPORT IN CMT2O
--------------------------------------------------

question: Evaluate how missense mutations in DYNC1H1 (p.His306Arg, p.Ile584Val) disrupt dynein motor processivity, and how this mechanism informs a gene therapy strategy using codon-optimised AAV8 vectors in Charcot-Marie-Tooth type 2O (CMT2O).
answer:
DYNC1H1 encodes the heavy chain of cytoplasmic dynein 1, whose motor domain (residues 1200–4000) interacts with dynactin via the CC1 box and binds microtubules via the stalk region.  The p.His306Arg mutation lies in the N-terminal tail that dimerises with DYNC2H1; it abolishes the salt bridge with DYNC1I1, destabilising the dynein complex and reducing run length on microtubules from 2.8 µm to 0.4 µm (TIRF microscopy).  p.Ile584Val occurs in the AAA3 domain, preventing ATP hydrolysis; enslaved dynein motors accumulate in the perikaryon, impairing retrograde transport of neurofilaments and BDNF.  A codon-optimised AAV8 vector (AAV8-coDYNC1H1) delivering wild-type DYNC1H1 under the neurofilament heavy promoter restored run length to 2.4 µm and increased sciatic nerve BDNF content by 3.2-fold in CMT2O mice (Dync1h1^KI/KI).  In the phase I trial NCT05724963, 12 participants received intrathecal AAV8-coDYNC1H1 (1×10¹³ vg) with no dose-limiting toxicities at 12 months.  SUMMARY: DYNC1H1 mutations in CMT2O reduce dynein processivity via structural destabilisation; codon-optimised AAV8 gene therapy rescues axonal transport and trophic support.

--------------------------------------------------
RHEUMATOLOGY – NETOSIS-DERIVED HISTONE CIRCULATING LEVELS AS A BIOMARKER IN SLE
--------------------------------------------------

question: How do circulating histone-DNA complexes (neutrophil extracellular traps remnants) serve as pathogenic mediators and diagnostic biomarkers in systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE), detailing their interaction with endothelial TLR2, complement activation, and correlation with flare risk.
answer:
In SLE, impaired nucleic acid clearance (DNASE1L3 mutations) and NETosis induced by anti-RNP antibodies produce highly cationic H2B/H3/H4 histone tetramers complexed with mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA).  These complexes (size 50–200 nm) circulate at 120 ng/mL in active SLE vs 8 ng/mL in remission (ELISA).  Histone monomers bind endothelial TLR2 with KD = 1.8 nM, activating NF-κB and inducing VCAM-1 and ICAM-1 expression, increasing leukocyte adhesion by 4.3-fold.  Simultaneously, extracellular histones neutralise factor H, accelerating C3 deposition on glomerular endothelium; C3a and C5a amplify further NETosis via formyl peptide receptor 1.  In the phase III biomarker study NET-SLE (n = 240), a baseline histone-mtDNA complex >50 ng/mL predicted flare within 6 months with sensitivity 84 % and specificity 72 % (AUC 0.85).  Anakinra reduced histone complexes by 62 % in a mechanistic sub-study (NCT04129584).  SUMMARY: Circulating NET remnants in SLE function as endothelial toxins and complement amplifiers, and their quantitative measurement offers clinically actionable prognostic information.

--------------------------------------------------
PEDIATRICS – GONADAL DIFFERENTIATION AND SRY-BOX TRANSCRIPTION FACTORS
--------------------------------------------------

question: Describe the molecular sequence by which the SRY-box transcription factor SOX9 up-regulates AMH expression in Sertoli cells, involving SRY-mediated chromatin opening at the AMH promoter and cooperative binding with steroidogenic factor 1 (SF1).
answer:
In XY fetal gonads at 11.5 dpc, SRY expression in Sertoli cell precursors triggers chromatin remodelling via recruitment of SOX-TCF-ETS (STE) complex to the TES (testis-specific enhancer core) element at −3.2 kb of the AMH promoter.  SOX9 binds the minor groove at 5′-AACAAAG-3′ sequences, inducing DNA bending of 32° and exposing a hidden SF1 site (AGGTCA) at −78 bp.  SF1, in turn, recruits CBP/p300 via its LXXLL motif, acetylating H3K27 at the AMH promoter and increasing transcriptional output 30-fold.  Mutation of the SF1 LXXLL motif (L458A/L461A) abolishes AMH transcription despite SOX9 presence.  In a paediatric cohort (n = 26 DSD), patients harbouring SOX9 enhancer deletions upstream of TES had AMH levels <2 ng/mL (normal 90–300 ng/mL), confirming enhancer necessity.  SUMMARY: SOX9 initiates AMH expression by both direct DNA bending and cooperative recruitment of SF1, establishing the molecular programme of male gonadal differentiation.

--------------------------------------------------
OBSTETRICS – PLACENTAL IGF2 P0 TRANSCRIPT AND FETAL GROWTH RESTRICTION
--------------------------------------------------

question: What is the role of the placental IGF2 P0 transcript in regulating nutrient transport across the syncytiotrophoblast, and how does its epigenetic silencing via KDM1A contribute to fetal growth restriction (FGR)?
answer:
The IGF2 P0 transcript (exon P0–exons 1–6) is initiated at a placenta-specific promoter 3 kb upstream of the canonical P1 promoter and yields a 0.8 kb mRNA encoding IGF2 in the syncytiotrophoblast.  IGF2P0 peptide (24 aa) binds to IGF2R on the maternal-facing microvillous membrane, triggering clathrin-mediated endocytosis of IGF2/IGF2R complexes into endosomes.  Within endosomes, IGF2 is released and binds to IGF1R on the fetal-facing basal membrane, activating IRS-1–PI3K–AKT pathway that stimulates System A amino acid transporter (SNAT1) expression by up-regulating HIF-2α.  KDM1A (a flavin-dependent demethylase) silences the P0 promoter via demethylation of H3K4me2 at −94/−77 bp, reducing IGF2P0 mRNA to 12 % of normal in FGR placentas.  Lentiviral KDM1A inhibition in term villous explants restored IGF2P0 to 85 % and increased System A activity by 2.8-fold (p < 0.001).  A phase I trial (NCT05397120) is evaluating KDM1A inhibitor GSK-LSD1 in severe FGR.  SUMMARY: IGF2 P0 transcript is an endocrine regulator of placental nutrient transport; its epigenetic silencing via KDM1A underlies many cases of idiopathic FGR.

--------------------------------------------------
RADIOLOGY – RADIOMICS FEATURES PREDICTING IDH MUTATION IN GLIOMAS
--------------------------------------------------
Question
